threadTitle: Things that instantly reveal you just moved here
body: You still call it the 'Overground' instead of just 'the train' and look lost trying to navigate the River Lea paths.
Caught the 59 and a guy in a full tuxedo foot-tapping to his own playlist. Brixton's nightlife truly never sleeps, even on the buses.

threadTitle: When you knew you were actually living here
body: The moment I saw an old man feeding pigeons outside a closed bakery, while a hipster on a bike swerved around him, I knew I was home.

threadTitle: Things Berlin does that nowhere else does
body: Only in Kreuzberg do you find a canal-side BBQ party with stray cats joining the picnic—locals and furballs bonding over grilled sausages.

threadTitle: Real signs someone is from here vs just working here
body: You can spot a local when they casually reference the BART delays like it’s weather gossip—'Did you hear the Civic Center’s closed again?'

threadTitle: Muni moments that define this city
body: Saw a guy juggling in front of the Muni stop, only to trip over the crack in the sidewalk as the bus pulled up—classic San Francisco chaos.

threadTitle: Things tourists get wrong about living here
body: Tourists think the ‘Barcelona chill’ is just about beaches; locals know it's about finding the best vermut on a Sunday afternoon at Plaça de la Vila.

threadTitle: How to spot a recent arrival
body: You can tell they're new if they still look baffled trying to decipher the tram schedule at Diagonal. Real locals know it's all about the bus.

threadTitle: Things that instantly reveal you just moved here
body: You still think the 149 bus takes you anywhere useful after 10 PM. Welcome to the club of late-night chaos and questionable kebab choices.
Last night on the 196, a guy in a bright orange tracksuit tried to sell his mixtape to a sleeping passenger. The mixtape was still in his hands when he got off at Tulse Hill.

threadTitle: When you knew you were actually living here
body: The moment I spotted a family of wild boars casually crossing the street on Sonnenallee. Just another day in Neukölln.

threadTitle: Things Berlin does that nowhere else does
body: You know those impromptu street concerts? And how the BVG buses just roll on by like it’s business as usual? Classic Berlin vibe.

threadTitle: Real signs someone is from here vs just working here
body: You know someone’s local when they can name at least three different taco trucks and passionately debate why La Taqueria has the best burrito.

threadTitle: Muni moments that define this city
body: Caught a Muni bus in the Tenderloin where a guy serenaded everyone with an out-of-tune ukulele. No one clapped, but the vibe was strangely uplifting.

threadTitle: Things tourists get wrong about living here
body: Tourists think the fountain at Plaça de la Vila is just for selfies, but locals use it as a meeting point for spontaneous jam sessions, especially on weekends.

threadTitle: How to spot a recent arrival
body: You can spot a newbie by their fresh, unwrinkled metro map—still clutching it like a lifeline. Locals just swipe their cards and go.
